title: Ball JSON Program
description:
JSON Schema (Draft 2020-12) for the Ball language wire format. Mirrors proto/ball/v1/ball.proto field-for-field, using the canonical protobuf-JSON mapping (see https://protobuf.dev/programming-guides/json/). The root of this schema validates a serialized ball.v1.Program — the shape every *.ball.json program file takes — optionally wrapped in a self-describing google.protobuf.Any envelope (a top-level "@type" key alongside the Program's own fields; this repo's encoders always emit the envelope). Other top-level Ball document kinds (a standalone Module, a BallManifest, a BallLockfile, a BallCapabilityReport) are not produced at the root of a *.ball.json program file but are still fully modeled under $defs for reuse — validate a document of one of those kinds against '#/$defs/<MessageName>' directly. See docs/BALL_JSON_SPEC.md for a narrative walkthrough with worked examples.

Protobuf-JSON rules that apply throughout this schema (see docs/BALL_JSON_SPEC.md #protobuf-json-rules for the full explanation):
  1. oneof fields: at most ONE of the variant keys is present on the object — there is no separate discriminator field. An entirely-unset oneof (zero variant keys present) is also legal proto3 JSON and is how Ball represents e.g. a `null` literal (an empty `Literal` object, `{}`).
  2. int64/uint64 scalars are serialized as decimal STRINGS (quoted), never bare JSON numbers, because JSON numbers cannot losslessly represent the full 64-bit range.
  3. `bytes` scalars are base64-encoded strings (standard alphabet, RFC 4648 §4).
  4. Enum values are serialized as their string constant name (e.g. "TYPE_STRING"), never the underlying integer.
  5. Fields holding their type's default/zero value (0, "", false, an empty list, an unset message) MAY be omitted entirely — proto3 JSON never requires emitting a default. The one exception is rule 1: a field inside a `oneof` is always emitted when that oneof case is selected, even if the value equals the type's default (this is how `intValue: "0"` is distinguished from the oneof being unset).
  Because of rule 5, this schema deliberately marks almost nothing as JSON-Schema `required` — omission is always valid protobuf-JSON. Every message schema instead sets `additionalProperties: false` so the schema's real value is catching typos/unknown fields and wrong field shapes, not enforcing presence.

Int64String.description: protobuf int64/uint64 — encoded as a decimal string per the protobuf-JSON spec (JSON numbers can silently lose precision above 2^53).

Int32.description: protobuf int32/uint32 — encoded as a bare JSON number (unlike int64/uint64, 32-bit values always fit in a JSON number losslessly).

Bytes.description: protobuf bytes — base64-encoded string (standard alphabet, RFC 4648 §4).

Double.description: protobuf double/float. Finite values are a bare JSON number; the protobuf-JSON spec additionally allows the special string tokens "NaN", "Infinity", and "-Infinity" because JSON numbers cannot represent them.

GoogleValue.description: google.protobuf.Value — the proto3-JSON mapping for Value is simply "any JSON value" (null, number, string, bool, object, or array).

GoogleStruct.description: google.protobuf.Struct — the proto3-JSON mapping is a plain JSON object whose values are google.protobuf.Value. Ball uses this as the universal "metadata" bag: cosmetic hints that a compiler may read but that must never change program semantics if stripped.

FieldDescriptorProtoType.description: google.protobuf.FieldDescriptorProto.Type — the wire/scalar type of a field.

FieldDescriptorProto.description: google.protobuf.FieldDescriptorProto — describes one field of a TypeDefinition.descriptor. Ball reuses protobuf's own descriptor types instead of inventing its own type system (see ball.proto's "Type System" section).

typeName.description: For TYPE_MESSAGE/TYPE_ENUM/TYPE_GROUP fields: the referenced type's name (may be a bare local TypeDefinition name like "main:Point", or a fully-qualified proto name like ".ball.v1.Expression").

DescriptorProto.description: google.protobuf.DescriptorProto — the field/type layout for a TypeDefinition. Ball never defines its own struct-field syntax; it always reuses this protobuf descriptor message, which every target language already knows how to map to native types.

Program.description: ball.v1.Program — top-level container for a complete Ball program. This is what a *.ball.json file's root object represents (this repo's encoders wrap it in a google.protobuf.Any envelope by adding a sibling "@type" key — see the schema-level description).

@type.description: Present only when this Program is serialized as a self-describing google.protobuf.Any envelope (type_url + inlined fields). Every fixture in tests/conformance/*.ball.json uses this form, e.g. "type.googleapis.com/ball.v1.Program". Absent when a Program is embedded as a plain, un-enveloped message.

entryModule.description: The module containing the entry point function.

entryFunction.description: The function to execute as the program's entry point.

Module.description: ball.v1.Module — a group of related type definitions and functions. Base modules (like "std") provide platform-specific implementations for is_base functions.

ModuleAsset.description: ball.v1.ModuleAsset — an embedded asset file (image, JSON fixture, template, etc.) carried alongside a module's code.

path.description: Relative path within the package (forward-slash separated).

mediaType.description: MIME type hint; empty = infer from file extension.

ModuleImport.description: ball.v1.ModuleImport — specifies how to resolve a module dependency. `source` is a oneof: at most one of http/file/inline/git/registry is present. All fixtures in this repo only set `name` (resolved by convention, e.g. "std"), leaving `source` unset.

name.description: Local alias used to reference this module in FunctionCall.module.

ModuleEncoding.description: ball.v1.ModuleEncoding — serialization format of a resolved module file.

InlineSource.description: ball.v1.InlineSource — `content` is a oneof: at most one of protoBytes/json.

json.description: A JSON-encoded ball.v1.Module string (i.e. the protobuf-JSON text of a Module, carried as a plain string value — not structurally validated by this schema).

version.description: Semver constraint, e.g. "^1.0.0". Empty = latest stable.

TypeRef.description: ball.v1.TypeRef — a structured, target-agnostic type reference, e.g. Box<int> -> {name:"Box", typeArgs:[{name:"int"}]}.

TypeDefinition.description: ball.v1.TypeDefinition — a named type. Replaces the old "_meta_Foo" function convention.

type.description: Type name; empty = infer from value.

FunctionDefinition.description: ball.v1.FunctionDefinition — a single-input, single-output function (gRPC-style). Base functions (isBase: true) have no `body`; their implementation is supplied per-platform by the target compiler/engine. A lambda is a FunctionDefinition with name == "".

Expression.description: ball.v1.Expression — the fundamental unit of computation. `expr` is a oneof over the 7 expression kinds; at most one of the 7 keys is present. All Ball code, in any target language, reduces to a tree of these.

FunctionCall.description: ball.v1.FunctionCall — calls a function with a single input expression.

Literal.description: ball.v1.Literal — `value` is a oneof over the scalar/list kinds. An entirely-empty Literal object (`{}`, no key present) is how Ball represents a null value — there is no dedicated null variant.

Reference.description: ball.v1.Reference — a named-variable reference. The special name "input" always means "this function's parameter".

MessageCreation.description: ball.v1.MessageCreation — constructs a new message instance (constructor call / struct literal). `typeName` empty = anonymous/inline (e.g. the synthetic input message of a base-function call).

Block.description: ball.v1.Block — sequential statements followed by a result expression, whose value is the block's value.

Statement.description: ball.v1.Statement — `stmt` is a oneof over let-binding vs. bare expression; at most one of the 2 keys is present.

BallManifest.description: ball.v1.BallManifest — a package manifest (analogous to pubspec.yaml / package.json / Cargo.toml). Serialized as ball.yaml (human-friendly) or ball.manifest.json (proto3 JSON). Not produced at the root of a *.ball.json program file — validate a manifest document against this $def directly.

BallLockfile.description: ball.v1.BallLockfile — pins every transitive dependency to an exact resolved version + content hash. Serialized as ball.lock.json (proto3 JSON).

ResolvedDependency.description: ball.v1.ResolvedDependency — `resolvedSource` is a oneof over http/git/file/registry; at most one of the 4 keys is present.

BallCapabilityReport.description: ball.v1.BallCapabilityReport — the output of `ball audit`: a structured, provably-complete report of every side effect a program can perform (every side effect flows through a named base function in a known module).
